work_unit: full deep audit of standalone autonomous-engine repo (hooks + watchdog + SKILL.md firmware)
situation: audit-cycle-state was cycle-complete after audit-042. All workspace git projects audited ≥1× except autonomous-engine standalone repo (distinct from autonomous-studio/.claude/hooks/ audited in audit-024). Triggered new full audit cycle targeting this unaudited repo.
action: Deep-read all 8 source files (6 Python hooks, watchdog.sh, SKILL.md). Identified 8 findings (2H/3M/2L/1I) covering shell injection surfaces, non-atomic writes, TOCTOU races, hardcoded Windows paths, env leakage, and unbounded message bodies. Wrote audit report audit-2026-07-08-043.md. Updated audit-cycle-state.json with 7 pending derived_fixes. No fix dispatched this round (audit-only per protocol).
outcome_evidence: audit-2026-07-08-043.md written with 8 findings across 8 source files; audit-cycle-state.json updated to status=fix-in-progress with 7 pending derived_fixes (AE-H-001, AE-H-002, AE-M-001..M-003, AE-L-001, AE-L-002); history entry appended.
next_suggestion: Dispatch AE-H-001 fix: save-checkpoint.py run() → list-form subprocess (no shell=True)
Dispatch AE-H-002 fix: watchdog.sh python3 -c → env var path passing
After H-fixes merged, dispatch M-fixes (atomic write, TOCTOU, Windows paths)

audit_findings.finding: run() uses shell=True subprocess — all current callers pass hardcoded strings but design invites future command injection
audit_findings.remediation: Convert to list-form subprocess.check_output(['git', 'branch', '--show-current'], ...) without shell=True; remove Windows % escape logic

audit_findings.finding: f-string embeds $LATEST bash variable into python3 -c source code — path with quotes/special chars breaks Python parser or enables code injection
audit_findings.remediation: Pass path via environment variable: LATEST_FILE="$LATEST" python3 -c "...os.environ['LATEST_FILE']..."

audit_findings.finding: write_checkpoint() uses direct open('w')+json.dump — non-atomic; crash leaves corrupt JSON. incremental-save.py already has correct tempfile+fsync+os.replace pattern
audit_findings.remediation: Reuse incremental-save.py atomic write pattern: tempfile.mkstemp → write → fsync → os.replace

audit_findings.finding: write_session_context() read-modify-write without lock — concurrent Stop hooks can lose session updates (TOCTOU race)
audit_findings.remediation: Use fcntl.flock for advisory locking, or tempfile+rename for atomic replace

audit_findings.finding: build_engine_firmware() hardcodes E:/x-tool/ Windows paths on lines 122,124 — engine recovery instructions point to nonexistent paths on Linux deployment
audit_findings.remediation: Replace hardcoded E:/x-tool/ with {PROJECT_DIR} f-string interpolation (partially done elsewhere in same function)

audit_findings.finding: subprocess inherits full parent os.environ including potential API keys/tokens — bootstrap.py is trusted local script but best practice is env whitelist
audit_findings.remediation: Pass only necessary env vars: PATH, PYTHONPATH, CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR

audit_findings.finding: ntfy.sh curl message body has no length cap — free tier 4KB limit silently truncates without error feedback
